basics.name: Jichun Zhao (Elliot Chiu)
basics.label: PhD Candidate, HIT Lab NZ, University of Canterbury
basics.summary: PhD Candidate at HIT Lab NZ, University of Canterbury, researching Human–Computer Interaction, XR Accessibility, Digital Cultural Heritage, Ageing, and Handheld Augmented Reality.

publications.name: Practical Approaches for the Protection and Inheritance of Intangible Cultural Heritage from the Perspective of Rural Revitalization: A Case Study of Xuzhou Mazhuang Sachets
publications.publisher: Journal of Xuzhou University of Technology (Social Sciences Edition)
publications.summary: Research on the protection and inheritance of intangible cultural heritage through the case study of Mazhuang Sachets under the perspective of rural revitalization.

Practical Approaches to the Inheritance and Protection of Intangible Cultural Heritage in the Context of Rural Revitalization: A Case Study of Mazhuang Sachets in Xuzhou, Jiangsu

portfolio.name: XR Accessibility
portfolio.description: Research on inclusive extended reality systems, interaction patterns, and evaluation methods that make immersive technologies more accessible.

portfolio.name: Handheld AR for Older Adults
portfolio.description: Exploring handheld augmented reality experiences that are usable, meaningful, and supportive for older adults.

portfolio.name: Digital Cultural Heritage
portfolio.description: Digital approaches for documenting, presenting, and sustaining cultural heritage through interactive and immersive media.

portfolio.name: Rewi Alley Museum AR
portfolio.description: An augmented reality project concept for museum interpretation and visitor engagement around Rewi Alley-related heritage.
